ip_outcome_note: Card name is the Pokemon's name. Bellsprout is Pokemon no. 069 in the National Pokedex, introduced in Generation I (Kanto). Internal Game Freak / Nintendo / The Pokemon Company IP — no external crossover risk. ip_verified set to true.

trivia_bullets.text: Bellsprout is the first card in the Battle Styles set — card no. 001/163 — making it the opening Grass-type common in this SWSH05 expansion. In Pokemon TCG card numbering, Grass-types typically lead the ordering because Grass is the first type in the game's elemental hierarchy. Being card 001 of a 163-card set means Bellsprout is the first card collectors encounter opening packs from this wave.
sources: Pokemon.com TCG Card Database: Battle Styles 001

trivia_bullets.text: Bellsprout is National Pokedex no. 069, a Grass/Poison-type introduced in Generation I (Red and Blue, 1996/1998). It is the first stage of a three-part evolution line: Bellsprout → Weepinbell (level 21) → Victreebel (Leaf Stone). Its flavor text references a real-world folk belief: mandrake roots (Mandragora officinarum) were traditionally thought to resemble human faces and scream when pulled from the ground. The card's flavor text reads: 'Its bud looks like a human face. Because of the bud, it is rumored to be a type of legendary mandrake plant.' This mythological grounding is consistent across multiple Pokedex entries for the species.

Flavor text: "Its bud looks like a human face. Because of the bud, it is rumored to be a type of legendary mandrake plant." The mandrake plant (Mandragora) has appeared in folklore from ancient Greece through medieval herbalism; its anthropomorphic root was believed to shriek when uprooted, a legend preserved in Harry Potter (Mandrake), Pan's Labyrinth (Mandrake root), and here — Bellsprout as a folkloric plant-creature. No speaker is credited, per standard Pokemon TCG flavor text format.

Bellsprout (BST 001) is a Grass-type Basic Pokemon, 50 HP, Common. Attack: Venoshock (Colorless) — 10+, deals 40 more damage if the opponent's Active Pokemon is Poisoned. Venoshock is drawn from the Generation V move (introduced in Black and White), which deals double damage when the target is poisoned. In the main-series games, Bellsprout cannot learn Venoshock by level-up — it requires a TM — making the TCG attack a mechanical borrowing rather than a class-natural ability. Illustrated by Sekio, who has been an active Pokemon TCG illustrator since the Guardians Rising set and has approximately 73 card credits.
